# UC-18.1.1 · Cisco ACI Fabric Health Score Monitoring (Topology, Tenant, EPG)

## Description

Tracks Cisco Application Centric Infrastructure fabric-wide and scoped APIC health scores anchored on Managed Object classes—**healthInst** children, fabricHealth statistics, **fabricNode** roles for controllers versus leaf/spine/virtual leaf nodes, **fvTenant** linkage for tenants, **fvAEPg** for endpoint groups, **fvCtx** for VRF contexts—while aligning Splunk thresholds with Cisco crash zones (Healthy ≥90, Degraded 70–89, Warning 50–69, Critical <50) and correlating **faultInst** severity mixes so teams spot policy-driven dips affecting tenants or EPGs before workloads violate latency expectations.

## Value

Fabric-wide collapse below the Healthy band frequently precedes **east-west** saturation or fault suppression storms minutes before ticketing cites database timeouts. Keeping topology scores, tenant or EPG regressions, and fault histograms inside one Splunk workspace collapses redundant bridges across **NetOps** and platform squads when optics noise or planned pod interconnect maintenance explains transient degradation.

## Implementation

Enable **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** syslog ingestion plus bespoke HTTPS collectors hitting **aaaLogin.json** and class endpoints for **healthInst**, **fabricHealth5min**, **fabricNode**, **fvTenant**, **fvAEPg**, **fvCtx**, **faultInst**; normalize sourcetypes to **cisco:aci:health** and **cisco:aci:fault**. Encode Cisco crash-zone thresholds into alerts and dashboards layering syslog mnemonics beneath REST-fed scores.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• APIC software in supported 6.0(x), 6.1(x), or 6.2(x) trains (legacy 5.x still observed—plan upgrades) with HTTPS reachability from Splunk collectors on 443/tcp and a dedicated read-only APIC automation account distinct from interactive admins.
• **NX-OS for ACI mode** 15.x / 16.x across leafs, spines, virtual leaf (vleaf), and tier-2-leaf roles where deployed—capture **fabricNode** role semantics so dashboards separate controllers from forwarding nodes.
• **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** (**Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**, Splunkbase 4022, 2.0.x commonly deployed in 2026) installed where syslog lands; remember this TA normalizes APIC and fabric syslog into sourcetype=**cisco:aci:syslog** only—it does not poll REST APIs by itself.
• Supplement with **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (rest_ta, Splunkbase 1546) or a Python modular/scripted input using requests plus **aaaLogin.json** / **aaaRefresh.json** token lifecycle (~10 minute TTL) for JSON pulls indexed as **cisco:aci:health** and **cisco:aci:fault**.
• Optional Cisco DC Networking Application for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777) when you want curated dashboards spanning Cisco TAs.
• RBAC: roles executing these searches must include the operational index (often cisco_aci) plus any HEC indexes used by pollers.

Step 1 — Configure ingestion aligned to MO coverage
(1) Syslog path (TA-native): steer APIC and leaf/spine events to Splunk TCP or UDP 514; confirm **Monitoring Destinations** syslog targets match firewall rules and that Cisco mnemonic faults (examples F0103 link down, F0105 LLDP change, F0613 BGP flap, F1394 TEP move, F2102 border-leaf leak) remain visible inside **cisco:aci:syslog** events for corroboration.
(2) REST polling path (custom): schedule HTTPS GET calls at 60–180s cadence against **/api/node/mo/topology/health.json** for the topology-wide score and parallel pulls against `/api/class/healthInst.json`, `/api/class/fabricHealth5min.json`, `/api/class/fabricNode.json`, `/api/class/fvTenant.json`, `/api/class/fvAEPg.json`, `/api/class/fvCtx.json`, `/api/class/faultInst.json` with rsp-subtree-include=health where you need parent-plus-health snapshots in one payload.
(3) Authentication uses POST `https://<apic>/api/aaaLogin.json` with {"aaaUser":{"attributes":{"name":"svc_aci_poll","pwd":"…"}}} capturing APIC-cookie plus token fields inside imdata; refresh via `/api/aaaRefresh.json` before TTL expiry—AAA backends may be local, RADIUS, TACACS+, LDAP, or Microsoft Entra ID SAML depending on operations policy.
(4) Normalize outbound JSON lines so Splunk extracts dn, classId, cur or healthScore, severity for **faultInst** rows, and fabric role identifiers matching APIC Managed Object naming for **fvTenant** / **fvAEPg** / **fvCtx** children.
(5) Crash-zone mapping for alerting follows Cisco Operations Guide bands: Healthy ≥90 green, Degraded 70–89 yellow, Warning 50–69 orange, Critical <50 red—**fvHealthScoreEvalPol** may tune weights but defaults derive composite scores from descendant **faultInst** defects weighted critical 90%, major 60%, minor 30%, warning 10%, info/cleared 0%.

Step 2 — Build Splunk correlation across topology, tenant, EPG, and VRF scopes
Execute once REST-fed sourcetypes populate index=cisco_aci; extend field aliases if pollers flatten attributes differently.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:health" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault") earliest=-24h
| eval mo_class=coalesce(classId, mo_class, object_type, "")
| eval cur_num=tonumber(coalesce(cur, healthScore, metric_value, score))
| eval dn_lc=lower(coalesce(dn, distinguished_name, ""))
| rex field=dn_lc max_match=1 "/pod-(?<pod_tag>\d+)/"
| eval scope_hint=case(
    mo_class IN ("fabricHealth5min","fabricHealthHist5min","fabricHealthInst"), "fabric_timeseries",
    mo_class IN ("fabricNode","topSystem"), "fabric_node",
    mo_class IN ("fvTenant"), "tenant_object",
    mo_class IN ("fvAEPg"), "epg_object",
    mo_class IN ("fvCtx"), "vrf_context",
    mo_class IN ("healthInst"), "health_inst_child",
    mo_class IN ("faultInst"), "fault_inst_object",
    like(dn_lc,"%topology/health%"), "topology_aggregate",
    1=1, "other_scope")
| eval crash_band=case(
    isnull(cur_num) AND sourcetype!="cisco:aci:fault", "unknown",
    cur_num>=90, "healthy_ge90",
    cur_num>=70, "degraded_70_89",
    cur_num>=50, "warning_50_69",
    cur_num<50, "critical_lt50",
    1=1, "fault_or_unknown")
| eval sev_bucket=lower(coalesce(severity, fault_severity, faultSeverity, ""))
| stats latest(cur_num) as latest_health,
        latest(crash_band) as band,
        latest(scope_hint) as scope_label,
        latest(mo_class) as mo_class_latest,
        latest(pod_tag) as pod_tag,
        values(sev_bucket) as fault_severity_vals,
        sum(eval(if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault",1,0))) as fault_events
    by dn_lc
| eval rollup_label=case(
    latest_health>=90, "Healthy (>=90)",
    latest_health>=70, "Degraded (70-89)",
    latest_health>=50, "Warning (50-69)",
    latest_health<50, "Critical (<50)",
    1=1, "Unknown_or_fault_trace")
| where isnull(latest_health) OR latest_health<90 OR fault_events>0 OR mvcount(fault_severity_vals)>0
| sort latest_health
| head 200
| table dn_lc, pod_tag, scope_label, mo_class_latest, latest_health, band, rollup_label, fault_events, fault_severity_vals
```

Field rationale: **fabricHealth5min** / **fabricHealthHist5min** / **fabricHealthInst** expose Cisco-standard statistic buckets—use five-minute grains for pager correlation and hourly grains when smoothing **ISSU** noise. **healthInst** anchors decode why tenant-only faults drag **fvTenant** scores without dragging spine totals; **fabricNode** ties leaf/spine/controller roles including APIC controllers versus Nexus 9300/9300-GX leaf silicon. **fvTenant**, **fvAEPg**, and **fvCtx** distinguish tenant-wide health crashes versus endpoint-group contracts versus VRF leaks referenced by DN segments inside dn_lc. **faultInst** pairing explains histogram deltas even when raw health scores plateau—critical versus minor mixes spotlight mis-cabled optics versus benign policy churn.

Step 3 — Validate against APIC dashboards
(1) Compare Splunk latest_health versus APIC GUI health scores on identical **Distinguished Names**—differences wider than one poll interval hint duplicated ingestion or stale tokens.
(2) For **fvAEPg** anomalies, pivot into Tenants → Networking → EPGs and verify endpoint counts align with syslog floods.
(3) Cross-check **faultInst** severity stacks against APIC Faults browser filtered by code—Splunk mv fields should mirror Cisco severity vocab.
(4) **Multi-pod** fabrics: confirm pod_hint splits replicate Cisco pod identifiers shown under System → Controllers.
(5) Capture screenshot evidence whenever Splunk alerts precede GUI rendering—often aaaRefresh misses or overlapping collector intervals.

Step 4 — Operationalise dashboards and routing
(1) Present topology gauge tiles beside tenant and EPG tables ranked by lowest latest_health.
(2) Tie crash_band transitions to paging policies—suppress **ISSU** hosts via CMDB maintenance tags carried via lookup enrichment.
(3) Route Warning-band sustained breaches to **NetOps** chat channels; Critical-band breaches include fault_events counts plus syslog mnemonic excerpts.
(4) Feed IT workflows referencing Cisco Operations Guide terminology so executives align verbal severity with telemetry.
(5) Rotate svc_aci_poll secrets quarterly—failed aaaRefresh manifests as silent duplication or stale dn inventories.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• Zero **cisco:aci:health** events means REST collectors never authenticated—inspect splunkd.log on Heavy Forwarders for HTTP 401/403 versus TLS handshake failures against the APIC VIP.

• mo_class blank strings imply pollers emit lowercase keys—extend coalesce(classId, class, mo_class) after validating raw JSON samples.

• cur_num null throughout suggests extraction stripped numeric strings—wrap tonumber(replace(cur,"[^0-9\\.]","")) cautiously once vendor confirms formatting.

• Scores freeze while syslog escalates indicates divergent transports—compare indexer _indextime skew versus APIC clock drift beyond NTP tolerance.

• Crash-zone mismatch versus GUI typically traces to rounding—Splunk tonumber drops decimals while APIC UI rounds—standardize rounding before thresholds.

• **fvTenant** alarms without **fvCtx** context risk misrouting—join vrf hints via supplemental **fvCtx** poll lines keyed by DN ancestry.

• fabric_node rows duplicated across pods signals missing pod segmentation—audit dn_lc filters for duplicated tenants replicated via **Multi-Site**.

• REST pollers returning HTTP 429 mean APIC protection throttled you—increase intervals or shard classes across modular inputs.

• fault_events spikes without score drops hint acknowledgement backlog—inspect cleared faults versus active faults windows inside APIC.

• Webhook plus REST duplication doubles severity counts—dedup using **faultInst** occurrence timestamps plus changeSet identifiers when exported.

• Service accounts locked via Entra SAML misconfiguration mimic generic TLS failures—verify IdP signing certs alongside Splunk collector logs.


## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:health" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault") earliest=-24h
| eval mo_class=coalesce(classId, mo_class, object_type, "")
| eval cur_num=tonumber(coalesce(cur, healthScore, metric_value, score))
| eval dn_lc=lower(coalesce(dn, distinguished_name, ""))
| rex field=dn_lc max_match=1 "/pod-(?<pod_tag>\d+)/"
| eval scope_hint=case(
    mo_class IN ("fabricHealth5min","fabricHealthHist5min","fabricHealthInst"), "fabric_timeseries",
    mo_class IN ("fabricNode","topSystem"), "fabric_node",
    mo_class IN ("fvTenant"), "tenant_object",
    mo_class IN ("fvAEPg"), "epg_object",
    mo_class IN ("fvCtx"), "vrf_context",
    mo_class IN ("healthInst"), "health_inst_child",
    mo_class IN ("faultInst"), "fault_inst_object",
    like(dn_lc,"%topology/health%"), "topology_aggregate",
    1=1, "other_scope")
| eval crash_band=case(
    isnull(cur_num) AND sourcetype!="cisco:aci:fault", "unknown",
    cur_num>=90, "healthy_ge90",
    cur_num>=70, "degraded_70_89",
    cur_num>=50, "warning_50_69",
    cur_num<50, "critical_lt50",
    1=1, "fault_or_unknown")
| eval sev_bucket=lower(coalesce(severity, fault_severity, faultSeverity, ""))
| stats latest(cur_num) as latest_health,
        latest(crash_band) as band,
        latest(scope_hint) as scope_label,
        latest(mo_class) as mo_class_latest,
        latest(pod_tag) as pod_tag,
        values(sev_bucket) as fault_severity_vals,
        sum(eval(if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault",1,0))) as fault_events
    by dn_lc
| eval rollup_label=case(
    latest_health>=90, "Healthy (>=90)",
    latest_health>=70, "Degraded (70-89)",
    latest_health>=50, "Warning (50-69)",
    latest_health<50, "Critical (<50)",
    1=1, "Unknown_or_fault_trace")
| where isnull(latest_health) OR latest_health<90 OR fault_events>0 OR mvcount(fault_severity_vals)>0
| sort latest_health
| head 200
| table dn_lc, pod_tag, scope_label, mo_class_latest, latest_health, band, rollup_label, fault_events, fault_severity_vals
```

## Visualization

(1) Radial gauge for topology aggregate pulled from **/api/node/mo/topology/health.json**. (2) Heatmap crash_band versus scope_label fabric versus tenant versus EPG versus VRF. (3) Timechart **fabricHealthHist5min** smoothed scores per fabric_node role. (4) Stacked severity charts from fault_severity_vals per dn_lc. (5) Tabular worst-latest_health leaderboard with mo_class_latest and fault_events for ticketing drilldown.
